description: A clean, document-style statistical infographic on a white background using black serif headings and bullet text, paired with NAEP (National Assessment of Educational Progress) horizontal stacked bar charts in shades of blue and gray. The top section, headed '12th Grade Math', states: 'Just 22% of 12th graders are proficient in math, meaning 78% are not proficient.' and 'In 2024, the average grade 12 mathematics score was the lowest it has been since the current assessment began in 2005.' Below is a chart titled 'Trend in twelfth-grade NAEP mathematics achievement-level results' with rows for years 2024, 2019, 2015, 2013, 2009, 2005, each segmented into 'Below NAEP Basic', 'NAEP Basic', and 'At or above NAEP Proficient' percentages (e.g., 2024: 45 / 33 / 22). The lower section, headed '12th Grade Reading', states: '35% of 12th graders are proficient in reading, meaning 65% are not proficient.' and 'The average grade 12 reading score was lower in 2024 than in all previous assessments.', followed by a parallel reading-results chart for years 2024, 2019, 2015, 1992. The style is sober and data-forward (it appears to reproduce official NAEP report figures) rather than a flashy meme. The messaging uses declining education statistics to argue, per the tweet, that the 'failed' federal education bureaucracy must be eliminated and education/workforce programs moved closer to the states under POTUS. Target audience is policy-minded readers and the public being persuaded to support dismantling the Department of Education.
notable_text: 12th Grade Math — Just 22% of 12th graders are proficient in math, meaning 78% are not proficient; 12th Grade Reading — 35% of 12th graders are proficient in reading, meaning 65% are not proficient
